texto: Elizabeth was so sweet this afternoon trying to show P.B. his sitting room. He became absorbed in some jungle prints along the passage and would not come. The corners of her mouth went down after the third attempt & putting both hands on his shoulders she said angelically: ‘Bertie do listen to me.’ He kissed her and came at once.
explicacao: Orientação direta de William Shawcross sobre temas ligados a amor. A afirmação “Elizabeth was so sweet this afternoon trying to show P.B. his sitting room. He became absorbed in…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, listening, love, útil em momentos de casamento e love.

frase_original: Elizabeth was so sweet this afternoon trying to show P.B. his sitting room. He became absorbed in some jungle prints along the passage and would not come. The corners of her mouth went down after the third attempt & putting both hands on his shoulders she said angelically: ‘Bertie do listen to me.’ He kissed her and came at once.

texto: After she married the Duke of York, she immediately transformed his life, bringing him love, understanding, sympathy and support for which he had always craved. She inspired him, she calmed him and she enabled him for the first time in his life to believe in himself. Her sense of humor awoke his own, her natural gaiety lightened him. Their marriage was a rare union in which each complemented and enhanced the other.
explicacao: Citação de William Shawcross sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “After she married the Duke of York, she immediately transformed his life, bringing him love, unde…” articula motivacao, marriage, royal-family em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e casamento.
